book_claims.claim: Red meat is explicitly excluded from the Longevity Diet; associated with increased cancer and cardiovascular disease risk
book_claims.mechanism: High leucine, saturated fat, heme iron; elevates IGF-1 and TOR-S6K; pro-inflammatory
book_claims.study_ref: ch4-13 — Fung et al., Annals of Internal Medicine, 2010

book_claims.claim: High intake of red meat correlates with elevated risk of heart disease in women
book_claims.mechanism: High animal-based protein and saturated fat content raise cardiovascular risk

book_claims.claim: A low-carbohydrate, mostly animal-based, high-protein diet doubles the risk of death from any cause and raises cardiovascular disease death risk by 40%
book_claims.mechanism: Animal protein raises IGF-1 and promotes inflammation; saturated fat increases LDL cholesterol

supplementary_research.finding: Fung et al. (2010) confirmed: low-carb animal-protein diet associated with 2× higher all-cause mortality; plant-based low-carb diet not associated with increased risk
Pan et al. (2012) NEJM: each additional daily serving of red meat associated with 13% increased all-cause mortality; processed meat 20%

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highly bioavailable but excessive heme iron linked to oxidative stress and colorectal cancer
Raises LDL; no recommended intake in Longevity Diet
mTOR/IGF-1 activator; high levels associated with accelerated aging in preclinical studies

synergies.description: Legumes are the recommended plant-protein replacement for red meat in the Longevity Diet
Fish is the only recommended animal protein; red meat should be eliminated
Traditional pairing but does not mitigate cardiovascular or cancer risks attributed to red meat
